metadata.title: 11th Pass FinOps Execution — Migrate to europe-west4, Zero Egress
metadata.approach: Blue-green migration: create new stack in europe-west4, verify, cutover, destroy old
metadata.estimated-time: 3-4 hours (includes data copy, Docker push, Pulumi deploy)
metadata.key-constraint: MUST use blue-green migration. Pulumi will DELETE old GCS buckets (force_destroy=True) and Cloud SQL (deletion_protection=False) when region changes. Data loss is guaranteed if we do a naive `pulumi up` with region change.

context.problem: GAR is in europe-north1 (Finland) which has ZERO L4 GPUs. Every Docker pull (6.4 GB) is cross-region or intercontinental egress. March 2026 bill: €89.58 in egress alone. Same-region pulls are FREE. Moving to europe-west4 (Netherlands, 3 L4 zones) = €0 egress.
context.billing-data: Intercontinental egress (Excl Oceania): €57.71 (850 GB) Europe-to-Europe cross-region egress: €31.87 (1,879 GB) Storage: €1.23 (15 GB-months) N4 Controller (Belgium, 380h): €16.08 L4 GPU Spot (Seoul, 69h): €9.10 L4 GPU Spot (Netherlands, 38.65h): €8.63
context.target-region: europe-west4 (Netherlands): - 3 L4 zones (best EU availability) - L4 spot: $0.340/hr | L4 on-demand: $0.742/hr - GAR, Cloud SQL, Cloud Run, GCS all supported - Already #1 priority in our europe.yaml region config
context.reviewer-warnings: CRITICAL: Pulumi will DELETE old buckets (force_destroy=True) when region changes. CRITICAL: GCS bucket location is IMMUTABLE — Pulumi does delete-then-create replacement. CRITICAL: Cloud SQL region is IMMUTABLE — same delete-then-create behavior. CRITICAL: hpo_grid_worker.yaml has HARDCODED region: europe-north1 (line 35). 30+ files reference europe-north1 across deployment, configs, tests, scripts, KG, docs.

phase.name: Pre-Migration Verification
phase.description: Verify europe-west4 has all required quotas before touching anything.

task.title: Verify europe-west4 GPU + CPU quotas
task.commands:
# Check L4 GPU quota in europe-west4 gcloud compute regions describe europe-west4 --project=minivess-mlops \ --format="table(quotas.filter('metric:NVIDIA_L4_GPUS').extract(limit,usage))"
# Check N4/E2 CPU quota (for controller) gcloud compute regions describe europe-west4 --project=minivess-mlops \ --format="table(quotas.filter('metric:CPUS').extract(limit,usage))"
# Verify SkyPilot can see L4 in europe-west4 uv run sky show-gpus L4 --cloud gcp 2>&1 | grep europe-west4
task.verification: L4 quota > 0, CPU quota >= 8 (controller + 1 training VM)
task.failure-action: If quota is 0, request increase via GCP Console before proceeding.

task.title: Inventory current data sizes for copy estimation
task.commands: gsutil du -s gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data/ gsutil du -s gs://minivess-mlops-mlflow-artifacts/ gsutil du -s gs://minivess-mlops-checkpoints/
task.note: Estimate copy time. DVC data is ~3 GB. MLflow artifacts may be larger.

phase.name: Data Backup — Before Any Infrastructure Changes
phase.description: Copy ALL GCS data to new region-specific buckets. This MUST complete before any Pulumi changes because Pulumi will delete the old buckets.

task.title: Create new GCS buckets in europe-west4 and copy data
task.description:
Create new buckets with -ew4 suffix, copy data. Keep old buckets untouched. The -ew4 suffix is temporary — after migration, Pulumi will create the canonical bucket names in europe-west4.
NOTE: We may be able to KEEP the same bucket names if we use a separate Pulumi stack (blue-green). GCS buckets are globally unique but region is set at creation. If we create the new stack BEFORE destroying the old, we need different names. After old stack is destroyed, we could re-create with the original names — but that risks a race condition.
DECISION: Use the same bucket names. Do NOT create new buckets manually. Instead, use the blue-green Pulumi stack approach: 1. Old stack (dev) keeps europe-north1 buckets alive 2. New stack (dev-ew4) creates europe-west4 buckets with -ew4 suffix 3. Copy data from old to new 4. Update all configs to point to -ew4 buckets 5. Destroy old stack → original names become available 6. (Optional later) Rename -ew4 buckets back to original names
SIMPLER ALTERNATIVE: Since this is a greenfield project with 2 weeks of data, and DVC data comes from a public S3 bucket, we can just: 1. Create new GAR repo in europe-west4 (manually or via new Pulumi stack) 2. Push Docker images 3. Let SkyPilot re-pull DVC data from S3 origin on first job 4. Destroy old europe-north1 Pulumi stack 5. Create fresh europe-west4 stack with original bucket names
task.decision:
For a greenfield academic project with 2 weeks of data: - DVC data: re-pull from S3 origin (automatic in SkyPilot setup) - MLflow artifacts: all checkpoints were LOST due to 413 anyway (zero useful data) - Cloud SQL: disabled (enable_cloud_run=false, no useful experiment data) - GAR images: re-push from local Docker cache
VERDICT: Clean slate. No data migration needed. Destroy old, create new.

phase.name: Pulumi Infrastructure Migration
phase.description: Destroy old europe-north1 stack, create fresh europe-west4 stack. Clean slate — no data migration needed (greenfield project).

task.title: Make region a Pulumi config variable (reproducible region changes)
task.description:
The Pulumi code ALREADY uses `region = config.get("region") or "europe-north1"` (line 30). All resources use this variable. The only hardcoded references are: - Comments/docstrings (cosmetic) - Pulumi.yaml default value - Pulumi.dev.yaml stack config
To change region, we ONLY need to update Pulumi.dev.yaml and Pulumi.yaml. The code is already parameterized. This is the "easy to change region" design.
task.changes: Pulumi.yaml: Change default region to europe-west4, update description Pulumi.dev.yaml: Change gcp:region to europe-west4 __main__.py: Update default fallback in line 30, update docstring/comments

task.title: Downsize controller: n4-standard-4 → e2-medium, move to europe-west4
task.changes: .sky.yaml: infra: gcp/europe-west1 → gcp/europe-west4 cpus: 4+ → 2+ Add: memory: 4+ (This targets e2-medium: 2 vCPU, 4 GB, ~$0.034/hr vs $0.169/hr)
task.verification: uv run sky jobs launch --dryrun ... to verify controller config resolves correctly

task.title: Destroy old Pulumi stack (europe-north1 resources)
task.description: This destroys ALL europe-north1 resources: GCS buckets, GAR repo, Cloud SQL. Safe because: greenfield project, all checkpoints already lost to 413, DVC data re-pullable from S3 origin, Cloud SQL was disabled.
task.commands: cd deployment/pulumi/gcp pulumi destroy --yes # Verify: no resources remain pulumi stack export | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(len(d.get('deployment',{}).get('resources',[])),'resources remaining')"
task.warning: This deletes ALL GCS data. Confirm no useful data exists.

task.title: Deploy new Pulumi stack (europe-west4 resources)
task.commands: cd deployment/pulumi/gcp pulumi up --yes # This creates: GCS buckets, GAR repo, Cloud SQL (if enabled), Cloud Run (if enabled) # All in europe-west4
task.verification: pulumi stack output docker_registry # Must show: europe-west4-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess gsutil ls gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data/ # Must succeed (empty bucket in europe-west4)

task.title: Push Docker images to new GAR in europe-west4
task.commands:
# Authenticate to new GAR gcloud auth configure-docker europe-west4-docker.pkg.dev
# Tag and push base image docker tag minivess-base:latest \ europe-west4-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/base:latest docker push europe-west4-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/base:latest
# Tag and push MLflow image (if needed for Cloud Run later) docker tag minivess-mlflow:latest \ europe-west4-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/mlflow:v3.10.0 docker push europe-west4-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/mlflow:v3.10.0
task.verification: gcloud artifacts docker images list \ europe-west4-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess \ --include-tags # Must show base:latest and mlflow:v3.10.0
task.note: ~30-60 min for 6.4 GB upload on residential internet

phase.name: Code Changes — Update All Region References
phase.description: Update all 30+ files referencing europe-north1. Single atomic commit. Uses /self-learning-iterative-coder for the test file updates.

task.title: Create europe_strict.yaml region config (co-located, zero egress)
task.description: New region config that pins ALL jobs to europe-west4 only. Zero egress. Spot by default, on-demand fallback available.
task.content: # Strict same-region config — ALL jobs in europe-west4 (co-located with GAR + GCS). # Zero cross-region egress. Use for debug runs and cost-sensitive experiments. # For broader availability, use europe.yaml (allows cross-EU) or europe_us.yaml. description: "Single-region europe-west4 — zero egress, co-located with all GCP services" regions: gcp: L4: - region: europe-west4 zones: 3

task.title: Update SkyPilot YAMLs (5 files) — GAR image_id to europe-west4
task.changes: ALL: image_id: docker:europe-north1-docker.pkg.dev/... → docker:europe-west4-docker.pkg.dev/... hpo_grid_worker.yaml ALSO: region: europe-north1 → europe-west4 (line 35) ALL: update comments referencing europe-north1

task.title: Update config files (3 files)
task.changes: gar.yaml: server + image_prefix → europe-west4-docker.pkg.dev gcp_spot.yaml: region → europe-west4, docker_image → europe-west4-docker.pkg.dev/... debug.yaml: region_config: europe_us → europe_strict (zero egress for debug)

task.title: Update region comments in europe.yaml and europe_us.yaml
task.changes: Update comments: "closest to GCS europe-north1" → "closest to GCS europe-west4" Update descriptions referencing europe-north1

task.title: Update scripts (2 files)
task.changes: preflight_gcp.py line 37: GAR_IMAGE → europe-west4-docker.pkg.dev/... preflight_gcp.py: CPU quota check region → europe-west4 run_factorial.sh: comment with GAR path

task.title: Update tests (4+ files) — TDD via /self-learning-iterative-coder
task.changes: All: assert "europe-north1-docker.pkg.dev" → assert "europe-west4-docker.pkg.dev" BETTER: Read region from configs/registry/gar.yaml (single source of truth) instead of hardcoding ANY region in test assertions.
task.design-improvement: Tests should NOT hardcode the region. Instead: 1. Read configs/registry/gar.yaml to get canonical GAR server 2. Assert SkyPilot YAMLs match the canonical config 3. This way, changing region = changing ONE file, tests auto-adapt

task.title: Update governance and knowledge graph files
task.changes: All: europe-north1 → europe-west4 where referring to current infrastructure

task.title: Run make test-staging — verify all tests pass
Create FinOps shift-left test suite
task.description: New test file: tests/v2/unit/finops/test_cost_governance.py Deterministic pytest checks that prevent cost regressions. Key tests: - GAR region matches primary GPU region (would have prevented €89 bill) - Debug experiments use europe_strict region config - Controller instance type is cost-bounded - Experiment XML declares max duration and cost budget - No intercontinental fallback in debug region config

phase.name: Runtime Verification — Smoke Test in europe-west4

task.title: Launch DynUNet smoke test in europe-west4
task.commands: cd deployment/skypilot uv run sky jobs launch train_factorial.yaml \ --env MODEL_FAMILY=dynunet \ --env LOSS_NAME=dice_ce \ --env WITH_AUX_CALIB=false \ --env FOLD_ID=0 \ --env MAX_EPOCHS=2 \ --env EXPERIMENT_NAME=test_europe_west4 \ -y

verification.criterion: Job runs in europe-west4 (check sky jobs queue region)
Docker pull is same-region (check logs for pull time < 60s)
No GAR egress charges (check billing next day)

task.title: Verify controller launched in europe-west4 as e2-medium
task.commands: uv run sky status gcloud compute instances list --project=minivess-mlops \ --filter="zone:europe-west4-*" --format="table(name,machineType,zone,status)"

verification.criterion: Machine type is e2-medium or smaller (not n4-standard-4)

task.title: Delete old stale GAR images from europe-north1 (if any remain)
task.commands: # After old Pulumi stack is destroyed, the GAR repo should be gone. # Verify: gcloud artifacts repositories list --project=minivess-mlops # Should only show europe-west4 repo, no europe-north1

verification.gate: make test-staging — all tests pass with europe-west4 references
Smoke test completes in europe-west4 with same-region pull
Next billing cycle shows €0 GAR egress (or near-zero)
Controller runs as e2-medium in europe-west4

plan.design-principle:
After this migration, changing region again requires: 1. Edit Pulumi.dev.yaml: gcp:region → new_region 2. Edit configs/registry/gar.yaml: server + image_prefix → new_region 3. Run: pulumi up (creates new resources, potentially destroys old) 4. Push Docker images to new GAR 5. Tests auto-adapt (they read from gar.yaml, not hardcoded region)
Total effort for future region change: ~30 min (Pulumi + Docker push). No SkyPilot YAML changes needed if they read from the registry config.
plan.cost-projection: Current monthly: €15.06 (€89 egress + €16 controller + rest) After migration: GAR egress: €0 (same-region, free) GAR storage: ~€1 (with cleanup policy) Controller: ~€4 (e2-medium, 4h/day with autostop) GPU compute: ~€18 (same spot usage, just in europe-west4) Cloud Storage: ~€1.50 (GCS buckets, productive) Cloud SQL: €0 (disabled, gated on enable_cloud_run) Cloud Run: €0 (disabled) Total: ~€3.57/month (76% reduction)
